1	\chapter{Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf ($i=0$)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_FlatBaseChange}
3	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
4	% base change, I", and Tag 02KH (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex and
5	% references/stacks-coherent.md).
6	
7	\section{Setup and motivation}
8	
9	This chapter establishes the $i=0$ (i.e.\ \(H^0\) / direct-image) case of flat
10	base change: the formation of the pushforward \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) of a
11	quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat base change on the target. It is the
12	foundational, fully self-contained slice of the cohomology-and-base-change
13	package that ultimately underlies relative representability statements; the
14	higher derived cases \(R^i f_*\) for \(i \geq 1\) are treated separately and are
15	out of scope here.
16	
17	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
18	quasi-separated, and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module.
19	A base change of \(f\) along an arbitrary morphism \(g : S' \to S\) is recorded
20	by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
21	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
22	\[
23	  \begin{array}{ccc}
24	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
25	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
26	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
27	  \end{array}
28	\]
29	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), and \(g' : X' \to X\) is the
30	first projection. We write \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) for the pullback
31	of \(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
32	
33	\section{The canonical base-change map}
34	
35	\begin{definition}[Base-change map for the pushforward]
36	  \label{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
37	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap}
38	  \uses{}
39	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Section "Cohomology and
40	  % base change, I", base-change diagram (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
41	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L877--904).
42	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
43	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent sheaf on $X$.
44	  % Suppose further that $g : S' \to S$ is any morphism of schemes. Denote
45	  % $X' = X_{S'} = S' \times_S X$ the base change of $X$ and denote
46	  % $f' : X' \to S'$ the base change of $f$.
47	  % Also write $g' : X' \to X$ the projection,
48	  % and set $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$."
49	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S\,Cohomology and base
50	  change, I.}
51	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
52	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(g : S' \to S\) an arbitrary morphism, giving the
53	  cartesian square above with projection \(g' : X' \to X\) and
54	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). The \emph{base-change map for the
55	  pushforward} is the canonical morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules
56	  \[
57	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \longrightarrow f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr)
58	    = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
59	  \]
60	  It is adjoint to the morphism
61	  \(f_*\mathcal{F} \to g_*\, f'_*\mathcal{F}' = f_*\,(g')_*\mathcal{F}'\) obtained
62	  by applying \(f_*\) to the unit
63	  \(\mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) of the
64	  \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction, using the commutativity
65	  \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\) of the square.
66	\end{definition}
67	
68	\section{The affine case}
69	
70	The whole result rests on the following elementary computation in the affine
71	setting, where the base-change map is visibly an isomorphism because tensoring is
72	associative.
73	
74	\begin{lemma}[Affine base change]
75	  \label{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
76	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso}
77	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
78	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
79	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I)
80	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L906--918).
81	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
82	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
83	  % Assume $f$ is affine.
84	  % In this case $f_*\mathcal{F} \cong Rf_*\mathcal{F}$ is
85	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf, and for every base change diagram
86	  % (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
87	  % we have
88	  % $$
89	  % g^*f_*\mathcal{F} = f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}.
90	  % $$"
91	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
92	  change''.}
93	  Assume \(f : X \to S\) is an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
94	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then for every base-change square as
95	  above the canonical map of
96	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
97	  \[
98	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\cong\; f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr).
99	  \]
100	\end{lemma}
101	
102	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The vanishing of higher direct images is
103	% Lemma \ref{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.
104	% The statement is local on $S$ and $S'$. Hence we may
105	% assume $X = \Spec(A)$, $S = \Spec(R)$,
106	% $S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$
107	% for some $A$-module $M$.
108	% We use Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-widetilde-pullback}
109	% to describe pullbacks and pushforwards of $\mathcal{F}$.
110	% Namely, $X' = \Spec(R' \otimes_R A)$ and
111	% $\mathcal{F}'$ is the quasi-coherent sheaf associated
112	% to $(R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M$.
113	% Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
114	% equality
115	% $$
116	% (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
117	% $$
118	% as $R'$-modules."
119	\begin{proof}
120	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map}
121	  The assertion is local on \(S\) and on \(S'\), so we may assume all schemes in
122	  sight are affine: \(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\),
123	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\), with \(f\) corresponding to a ring map
124	  \(R \to A\) and \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) the quasi-coherent sheaf
125	  associated to an \(A\)-module \(M\). Computing the fibre product gives
126	  \(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), and the pullback
127	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to
128	  the \((R' \otimes_R A)\)-module \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). Under the
129	  dictionary between quasi-coherent sheaves on an affine scheme and modules,
130	  pushforward along an affine morphism is restriction of scalars and pullback
131	  along \(g\) is \(- \otimes_R R'\). The base-change map of
132	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is therefore identified with
133	  the canonical \(R'\)-linear comparison map for the two ways of forming the
134	  iterated tensor product, and the claim reduces to the associativity isomorphism
135	  \[
136	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;=\; R' \otimes_R M
137	  \]
138	  of \(R'\)-modules, which is an isomorphism (no flatness is needed in the affine
139	  case).
140	\end{proof}
141	
142	\section{Flat base change for the pushforward}
143	
144	\begin{theorem}[Flat base change, $i=0$ case]
145	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}
146	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso}
147	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
148	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 02KH (Lemma "Flat base change", part for
149	  % $i = 0$) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947--970, and
150	  % references/stacks-coherent.md).
151	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
152	  % $$
153	  % \xymatrix{
154	  % X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
155	  % S' \ar[r]^g & S
156	  % }
157	  % $$
158	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
159	  % with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
160	  % Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
161	  % For any $i \geq 0$
162	  % \begin{enumerate}
163	  % \item the base change map of
164	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
165	  % is an isomorphism
166	  % $$
167	  % g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
168	  % $$
169	  % \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
170	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
171	  % \end{enumerate}"
172	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), the
173	  \(i = 0\) case.}
174	  Consider the cartesian square
175	  \[
176	    \begin{array}{ccc}
177	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
178	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
179	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
180	    \end{array}
181	  \]
182	  with \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and
183	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\)
184	  is quasi-compact and quasi-separated. Then the base-change map of
185	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
186	  \[
187	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
188	    f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
189	  \]
190	  Equivalently, in the affine situation \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
191	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map
192	  \[
193	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
194	    H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
195	  \]
196	  is an isomorphism, where \(X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)}
197	  \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) and \(\mathcal{F}_B\) is the pullback of
198	  \(\mathcal{F}\).
199	\end{theorem}
200	
201	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Having said this, part (1) follows from part (2). Namely,
202	% part (1) is local on $S'$ and hence we may assume $S$
203	% and $S'$ are affine. In other words, we have $S = \Spec(A)$
204	% and $S' = \Spec(B)$ as in (2).
205	% Then since $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is quasi-coherent
206	% (Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images}),
207	% it is the quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_S$-module associated to the
208	% $A$-module $H^0(S, R^if_*\mathcal{F}) = H^i(X, \mathcal{F})$
209	% (equality by
210	% Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}).
211	% Similarly, $R^if'_*\mathcal{F}'$ is the quasi-coherent
212	% $\mathcal{O}_{S'}$-module associated to the $B$-module
213	% $H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$. Since pullback by $g$ corresponds
214	% to $- \otimes_A B$ on modules
215	% (Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-widetilde-pullback})
216	% we see that it suffices to prove (2).
217	%
218	% Let $A \to B$ be a flat ring homomorphism.
219	% Let $X$ be a quasi-compact and quasi-separated scheme over $A$.
220	% Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
221	% Set $X_B = X \times_{\Spec(A)} \Spec(B)$ and denote
222	% $\mathcal{F}_B$ the pullback of $\mathcal{F}$.
223	% We are trying to show that the map
224	% $$
225	% H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow H^i(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
226	% $$
227	% (given by the reference in the statement of the lemma)
228	% is an isomorphism.
229	%
230	% In case $X$ is separated, choose an affine open covering
231	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$ and recall that
232	% $$
233	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F}),
234	% $$
235	% see
236	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}.
237	% If $\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \ldots \cup (U_t)_B$ we obtain
238	% by base change, then it is still the case that each $(U_i)_B$ is affine
239	% and that $X_B$ is separated. Thus we obtain
240	% $$
241	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
242	% $$
243	% We have the following relation between the {\v C}ech complexes
244	% $$
245	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
246	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
247	% $$
248	% as follows from
249	% Lemma \ref{lemma-affine-base-change}.
250	% Since $A \to B$ is flat, the same thing remains true on taking cohomology."
251	\begin{proof}
252	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
253	  \emph{Reduction to the affine target.} The statement is local on \(S'\), so we
254	  may assume \(S\) and \(S'\) are affine, say \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and
255	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat. Because \(f\) is
256	  quasi-compact and quasi-separated, \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent on
257	  \(S\); it is therefore the sheaf associated to the \(A\)-module
258	  \(H^0(S, f_*\mathcal{F}) = H^0(X, \mathcal{F})\). Likewise
259	  \(f'_*\mathcal{F}'\) is the sheaf on \(S'\) associated to the \(B\)-module
260	  \(H^0(X', \mathcal{F}')\). Since pullback along \(g\) corresponds to
261	  \(- \otimes_A B\) on modules, the base-change map becomes the comparison map of
262	  \(B\)-modules
263	  \[
264	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \longrightarrow H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B),
265	    \qquad X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)} \operatorname{Spec}(B),
266	  \]
267	  and it suffices to prove that this map is an isomorphism.
268	
269	  \medskip
270	  \emph{The separated case via {\v C}ech complexes.} Suppose first that \(X\) is
271	  separated over \(A\). Choose a finite affine open cover
272	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\); by separatedness all finite
273	  intersections \(U_{i_0 \dots i_p}\) are again affine, so {\v C}ech cohomology
274	  computes sheaf cohomology:
275	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})\). Base changing
276	  the cover yields an affine open cover
277	  \(\mathcal{U}_B : X_B = (U_1)_B \cup \dots \cup (U_t)_B\) of the again-separated
278	  \(X_B\), with the same property
279	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\). The
280	  affine case (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) identifies each
281	  term of the base-changed {\v C}ech complex with the original term tensored over
282	  \(A\) with \(B\); that is, there is an isomorphism of cochain complexes
283	  \[
284	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) \;\cong\;
285	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B .
286	  \]
287	  Because \(A \to B\) is flat, \(- \otimes_A B\) is an exact functor and hence
288	  commutes with the formation of cohomology of a complex; taking \(H^0\)
289	  (degree-zero cohomology) gives the desired isomorphism
290	  \(H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\).
291	
292	  \medskip
293	  \emph{The quasi-separated case.} In general \(X\) need only be quasi-separated.
294	  Choose a finite affine open cover \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\).
295	  Now the intersections \(U_{i_0 \dots i_p}\) are quasi-compact and separated
296	  rather than affine, but they fall under the separated case already treated, so
297	  flat base change holds for each of them. One then compares the two
298	  {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequences with \(E_2\)-pages
299	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}))\) and
300	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}_B))\), converging to
301	  \(H^{p+q}(X, \mathcal{F})\) and \(H^{p+q}(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\) respectively.
302	  The separated case provides the term-by-term identification
303	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F}_B)) =
304	  \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \underline{H}^q(\mathcal{F})) \otimes_A B\) on the
305	  \(E_2\)-pages, and flatness of \(A \to B\) propagates this isomorphism through
306	  the spectral sequences to the abutment. In particular, in degree \(0\) one
307	  obtains \(H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \cong H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)\), as
308	  required. (For the \(i = 0\) statement at hand, one may avoid the spectral
309	  sequence entirely and argue by Mayer--Vietoris induction on the number \(t\) of
310	  cover members, reducing each step to the separated case.)
311	\end{proof}
